question.text: The Public Announcement for a buyback must be signed by which of the following?

answer.text: Only the Managing Director and CFO
Manager/Secretary and at least two directors including the MD (if any)
The merchant banker and the company secretary only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Schedule II, the Public Announcement must be signed by the manager or secretary of the company and at least two directors of the company, including the Managing Director, if any. **The Trap:** "All directors" is overly broad — only a minimum of two directors are required. The merchant banker does not sign the PA; it is signed by company officials. **Cross-Exam Context:** Similar signatory requirements for offer documents are tested in NISM Series IX in the context of IPO/FPO prospectus filings. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Verify signatory authority early — if the MD position is vacant, ensure the board resolution specifies which two directors will sign the PA.
